event: Photograph of Saint Peter's Square in Rome
symbols: colonnade embracing the piazza — the Church's arms reaching out to embrace the faithful
obelisk — ancient monument repurposed as a Christian symbol at the heart of Christendom
description: An early sepia-toned photograph showing a partial view of Saint Peter's Square in Rome. The Bernini colonnade with its rows of massive Doric columns extends across the middle ground. A tall Egyptian obelisk rises at the left. Behind the colonnade, the Apostolic Palace (papal residence) rises as a large multi-story building with many windows. The photograph is slightly worn and mounted on dark card, characteristic of mid-19th century calotype or albumen prints. The square appears mostly empty with a few indistinct figures.

_meta.title: View of Saint Peter's square in Rome
